text.heading: NATIONAL BUSINESS AND TECHNICAL EXAMINATIONS BOARD
(NABTEB)
NATIONAL HEADQUARTERS,
IKPOBA HILL P.M.B. 1747, BENIN CITY, EDO STATE.
MANUAL FOR STATION OFFICERS
FOR NABTEB CERTIFICATE EXAMINATIONS
A. INTRODUCTION
text.p: The conduct of public examinations demands high fidelity quality control in order to ensure quality assurance in content and delivery. This comes up at various stages in the examination process, namely during the pre-examination, actual conduct and post-examination activities.
One of such important stages in the actual process of conducting an examination is at the post of the custodians.
text.heading: B. RATIONALE FOR STATION OFFICERS
text.p: At the custodians, question papers and answer scripts are held in trust for examination bodies. If not carefully handled the issuing of question papers and receiving of answer scripts can pose serious threats to the quality of the examinations at this level.
For example, there have been reports of;
(a) Delay in issuing out question papers by custodians who are very busy with their primary assignments.
(b) Some custodians being tempted by unscrupulous supervisors to issue out question papers which are not due on the Time Table.

(c) Failure of custodians and supervisors to faithfully and honestly fill the Movement Form for the collection of question papers and return of answer scripts thereby creating difficulty for the Board in resolving naughty cases where these records are to serve as pointers.

There is therefore the need to designate a person at each custodian whose duty it is to see to the timely issuance of question papers to and receipt of answer scripts from the supervisors, among other responsibilities.
For the purpose of NABTEB Certificate Examinations such a person is called a Station Officer.
text.heading: (C) FUNCTIONS OF STATION OFFICERS
text.p: Station Officers are to:
(1) Issue out question papers to supervisors and receive answer scripts from them.
(2) Ensure that no supervisor collects question papers earlier than necessary i.e not earlier than one hour before the commencement of the paper.
(3) Ensure that supervisors submit answer scripts to the custodians not later than one hour after the end of a paper.
(4) Check and ensure that parcels containing question papers are correct in number and are not tampered with.
(5) Ensure that answer scripts of each centre are kept in separate bags.
(6) Ensure that Mark sheets for Practical papers are returned to the Custodian by the supervisor.
(7) Ensure that all unused parcels of question papers are returned intact to the custodian.
(8) Ensure that the Movement Book is correctly filled by the Supervisors.
(9) Make replacement for any absentee supervisor if necessary and contact the Zonal Office or National headquarters immediately.

(10) Check the movement book to ensure that all Mark sheets in respect of the practical question papers collected have been returned. Where this has not been done, the affected supervisors must be directed to do so.
(11) Ensure that script envelopes are properly labeled before accepting them from the Supervisors.
(12) Ensure that arrangement is made to replace a Supervisor who may wish to discontinue with the supervision.
(13) Ensure that unused materials are parceled and returned to the Board.
text.heading: GUIDELINES TO STATION OFFICERS
text.p: (1) Question papers are to be released to supervisors not earlier than 1 hour before the commencement of each paper.
(2) All question papers released to Supervisors must be entered and signed for by the Supervisors in the movement book indicating time and date of collection.
(3) Station Officers are to use the delivery note with the Custodian to do stock taking of the question papers.
(4) Station officers should submit a comprehensive report on the station work to Test Administration department not later than 72 hours after the completion of the assignment.
(5) Ensure that supervisors submit their reports and claims on the last day of the examination as directed.
(6) Ensure that supervisors produce their letters of introduction before commencing official transaction with them.
(7) Station officers are to report at their respective custodians one and half hours before the commencement of the 1st paper of the day.
